description_long: This rifle features a robust black metal receiver and barrel assembly, paired with a wooden stock and foregrip that provide a classic, utilitarian appearance. The stock is fixed and made from a medium-brown wood, offering stability and traditional ergonomics. The barrel is relatively short and thick, with a prominent cylindrical muzzle device at the end, likely a flash hider. The rifle is equipped with basic iron sights, including a front post and a rear notch, both mounted directly on the barrel and receiver. The magazine is not externally visible, suggesting an internal or tubular magazine design. The overall construction suggests durability and functionality, suitable for urban environments where reliability is essential.
description: A black metal rifle with a fixed wooden stock and foregrip, featuring a short barrel with a flash hider and iron sights. The design is sturdy and utilitarian, with no visible external magazine.
description_view_0: Front view showing the muzzle with a flash hider and the narrow profile of the barrel and foregrip.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the rifle, the fixed wooden stock, foregrip, and the iron sights along the top.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the end of the wooden stock and the alignment of the iron sights.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same features as view 1, with clear visibility of the receiver and barrel assembly.
